1.11: Beloved, do not imitate what is evil but what is good. The one who does good is from God; the one who does evil has not seen God.
1.1: The elder to the beloved Gaius, whom I love in truth.
1.6: who bore witness to your love before the church. You will do well to send them on their way worthily of God.
1.2: Beloved, I pray that in all things you may prosper and be in health, even as your soul prospers.
1.5: Beloved, you act faithfully in whatever you do for the brothers, even though they are strangers to you,
1.10: Therefore, if I come, I will call attention to what he is doing, spreading malicious nonsense about us. And not being content with that, he himself refuses to receive the brothers, and he hinders those who want to do so and puts them out of the church.
1.3: For I rejoiced greatly when brothers came and testified about your truth, as you walk in the truth.
1.4: I have no greater joy than this: to hear that my children are walking in the truth.
1.12: Demetrius has been testified to by all, and by the truth itself. We also testify, and you know that our testimony is true.
1.8: Therefore we ought to support such men, so that we may be fellow workers with the truth.
1.13: I had many things to write to you, but I do not wish to write them to you with ink and pen.
1.9: I wrote something to the church, but Diotrephes, who loves to be first among them, does not accept what we say.
1.7: For they went out for the sake of the Name, accepting nothing from the Gentiles.
1.14: But I hope to see you soon, and we will speak face to face. Peace to you. The friends greet you. Greet the friends by name.
